tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto is first on the route — what if you knock out glasses shopping early and travel lighter?
tasks.reward: Gets the glasses question settled in the first 3 days so you can focus on temples and food.
nuggets: Kyoto has optical shops in Shijo district
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto. I'm arriving March 23, 2026 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses early in the trip. I need stores that can make glasses quickly (ideally same-day or next-day), accept foreign prescriptions, and are located near central Kyoto (we're staying near Shijo). Give me a shortlist of 3-5 stores with addresses, Google Maps links, typical turnaround times, and price ranges. Include both budget chains (JINS, Zoff) and any quality mid-range options.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Call insurance: 'Will you reimburse glasses bought in Japan?'
tasks.hook_line: You leave in 4 days — better know if insurance covers this before you're standing in a Tokyo shop.
tasks.reward: Either unlocks $400 savings or prevents a $1000 surprise when you get home.
nuggets: Most US vision insurance covers foreign purchases
Prescription valid 1-2 years depending on state
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out if I can get reimbursed for eyeglasses bought in Japan. I have US vision insurance (you can assume standard VSP or similar). I want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses during my trip March 23 - April 7. What do I need to ask my insurance company? What documentation do I need to save from the store? Are there any restrictions on foreign purchases? Give me a checklist of questions to ask and paperwork to collect.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'best temples Kyoto 2 days' and open 3 blogs
tasks.hook_line: You're landing in Osaka tomorrow and Kyoto is 2 days away — time to figure out what you're actually doing there.
tasks.reward: Turns Kyoto from a vague cloud into a concrete plan you can just execute when you arrive.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, good food, and a mix of major landmarks and less touristy spots. We're staying in central Kyoto (near Shijo). Give me a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route for each day. Include breakfast/lunch/dinner recommendations near each area. Keep it flexible — we don't want to be rushed.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Takayama itinerary 2 days' and bookmark top 3 guides
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the quiet mountain interlude between temple cities — what's the vibe there?
tasks.reward: Locks in the mountain town experience so you know what to pack and expect.
nuggets: Hida beef is the regional specialty
Shirakawa-go day trip possible from Takayama
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2 days in Takayama (March 28-29). Coming from Kyoto, heading to Tokyo after. We like walking, nature, local food, and quieter experiences after temple-heavy Kyoto. Should we do a day trip to Shirakawa-go? What else is worth doing in Takayama itself? Give me a rough 2-day plan with key sights, food recommendations, and any day trip logistics (bus times, booking needs). We want a slower pace here.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Tokyo 7 days first time itinerary' and save 3 guides
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive and you have 7 days — how do you not waste it wandering aimlessly?
tasks.reward: Turns Tokyo from overwhelming into structured with flex days built in for spontaneity.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 7 days in Tokyo (March 30 - April 6). Final leg of a 2-week Japan trip. We've already done heavy temple tourism in Kyoto, so Tokyo should balance iconic landmarks, neighborhoods to explore, good food, and maybe some modern/quirky Tokyo experiences. We're staying in Shibuya. Give me a rough day-by-day framework — not rigid, but structured enough that we're not decision-paralyzed each morning. Include neighborhood suggestions, any advance bookings needed, and food recommendations. We want a mix of planned activities and wandering time.

tasks.first_domino: Open Cerebro repo README and find 'Local Development' section
tasks.hook_line: This has been parked for a month — can you actually get the thing running or is it cursed?
tasks.reward: Unparks the entire Cerebro project and makes it something you can actively work on.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro project running locally. It's a developer portal for engineers at my company. I haven't touched it since late February and it's currently parked. I need to: 1) Clone or pull the latest version, 2) Install dependencies, 3) Configure any local environment variables, 4) Get the dev server running, 5) Verify I can access it in browser. Walk me through the setup step-by-step. If there are common errors (ports in use, missing config, database connections), help me troubleshoot them. Assume I'm on macOS with Node/npm installed.

recommended_slots.reason: Leaves in 4 days, Kyoto is first stop — quick research win to start the morning.
Insurance call is time-sensitive and unblocks $400+ potential savings before departure.
Kyoto is 2 days away and totally unplanned — this turns urgency into concrete action.
